title: Domain-native contrastive pretraining replaces the ImageNet trunk (corrected re-attempt)
hypothesis: Exp 41 diagnosed the real wall correctly but was rejected on compliance, not on merit: its implementation still carried the champion's backbone identifiers (mnv3s_features8.pt, mobilenet_v3_small) somewhere in model/, so the harness rejected it before training and the underlying scientific question -- does an ImageNet-classification trunk cap the field's ranking ability -- remains untested. Six straight reverts/rejections (36-41) all left the same thing untouched: an ImageNet-supervised MobileNetV3-Small trunk (mnv3s_features8.pt), unchanged in kind since exp 11, the only stage never once touched except by three reverted capacity-scaling attempts (8, 26, 28) that widened it without ever changing what it is trained to represent. That trunk's entire training signal is 'classify natural photographs of ImageNet objects'; it has never been told to treat six different lighting renders of the SAME place as the same thing, or to separate one map location's appearance from another's. The project's own diagnostic probes (runs/_probe_iter12_field.py, memory champion-failure-mode-probes) show the wall is exactly a discrimination failure: the true cell is the field's top-1 pick only ~2% of the time despite landing in its own top-20 (of 1024) on 33-58% of crops -- the descriptor narrows things down but cannot finish separating confusable lookalikes, and no downstream architecture/loss change (exps 34/36/37/39/40, all reverted) has fixed that because the descriptor handed to them was never trained to make that separation. This iteration replaces the ImageNet-pretrained trunk with a from-scratch trunk pretrained via self-supervised contrastive learning (NT-Xent) directly on the frozen relight pipeline's own multi-lighting, multi-heading renders of the TRAIN split: positive pairs = two independently-drawn views (different lighting bucket + different heading) of the SAME physical location; negatives = every other location in the batch. This is the identical scientific bet exp 41 made, run through a hardened implementation path this time: the champion's backbone identifiers are hard-deleted from model/model.py and model/train.py -- including the one place a plain code rewrite would NOT have caught them, a historical docstring sentence naming the old checkpoint path in prose, which backbonecheck.py scans verbatim since it deliberately does not strip docstrings (only '#' comments).
method: In model/model.py: delete `_build_pretrained_trunk`, the `mobilenet_v3_small` import, `PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH`, and the ImageNet mean/std buffers; replace the trunk with a brand-new from-scratch 4-stage plain conv stack (32->64->96->64 channels, stride 2 each, 128x128 -> 8x8x64, ~131k params, no borrowed weights, no checkpoint file). Delete the dark-expert head and brightness gate (exp 12); a single unified field head reads the new trunk's GAP + layout-squeeze descriptor, since the new trunk is pretrained specifically to already collapse lighting nuisance. Add a small discard-after-pretraining projection head. In model/train.py: add a new pretraining phase (nt_xent_loss + pretrain_contrastive) that runs before the existing supervised epoch loop, drawing paired lighting/heading views of the same locations from the pipeline's own cached renders and training the trunk+projector with an NT-Xent contrastive loss; the main supervised loop is otherwise unchanged except the trunk's fine-tune LR (no longer a delicately-borrowed pretrained model). Every literal occurrence of `mobilenet_v3_small` and `mnv3s_features8` -- including in prose, not just code -- is removed from both files; see implementation_brief for the exact grep-verifiable checklist.
expected_outcome: If the ImageNet-vs-domain-native descriptor gap is the real wall the last six rounds jointly point at, worst-case median error improves from the champion's 743.07 m to roughly 500-700 m (6-33%), with the clearest mechanism-level signature being the true-cell top-1 rate (matched-protocol iter-12 field probe) rising measurably above the champion's ~2% baseline -- direct evidence the pretraining objective bought real discriminative separation, not just a different starting point for the same eventual fit. Falsification forks: (a) if the metric is flat or regresses AND top-1 rate does not move, the descriptor-quality hypothesis is refuted at the pretraining-objective level -- the plain from-scratch trunk family or the modest ~150-step/128-location contrastive budget was too weak to learn anything the classifier head couldn't already extract from raw ImageNet features, redirecting the next iteration toward a longer/larger-batch pretraining budget or a reconstruction-style auxiliary (library L1) instead of contrastive; (b) if top-1 rate rises clearly but the primary metric does not follow, the new descriptor is more discriminative but something downstream fails to exploit it -- re-test with a lighting-conditional head restored on top of the new trunk before concluding the gate was dead weight; (c) if the metric regresses sharply while pretraining loss looks healthy, deleting the lighting gate was premature -- contrastive pretraining narrowed but did not eliminate the lighting gap the gate was compensating for, and the next iteration should keep the new trunk but restore a lighting-conditional head.
eli5: The model's 'eyes' were borrowed from a network trained to recognize everyday objects like cats and cars -- it was never taught that a rooftop at noon and the same rooftop at midnight are the same place, or that two different-looking places are different. This experiment throws that borrowed trunk away and grows a brand-new one from scratch, first playing a 'same place or different place?' matching game using nothing but the map images this project already generates in every lighting condition, before it ever tries to pinpoint a location. It's a bit like teaching someone to recognize your street by showing them photos of it at dawn and at midnight side by side, instead of handing them a general photo-classification textbook and hoping street-recognition falls out.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop is copied into its four 90° turns and all four travel through the same network side by side for the inference-time vote; the same turn-generating machinery now also supplies the random-heading views used by the new pretraining game below

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: a from-scratch, plain 4-stage conv stack (32->64->96->64 channels, no borrowed ImageNet weights, no MobileNetV3 family, no checkpoint file) that FIRST plays a 'same place or different place?' matching game against pairs of differently-lit, differently-turned views of the SAME map location drawn from the project's own renders, and only afterward is handed to the location-scoring stages below

stages.name: Layout summary
stages.detail: a slim extra layer condenses the new trunk's 8×8 feature grid into a spatial code that remembers what sits where in the crop, alongside its texture average -- same recipe as before, re-sized for the new trunk's channel count

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: REMOVED: the old day/night switch and its two separate scorers are deleted, since the new feature extractor is already taught to see through lighting changes during its matching game; the freed brightness reading is handed to Confidence instead

stages.name: Probability map
stages.detail: a single unified scorer (no more day/night blend) reads the layout summary directly into one heat map over the area

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: the same contrast-sharpened balance-point decode as before, now reading a single unified heat map instead of a blended two-expert one -- the sharpening math is unchanged, but what it decodes is structurally simpler

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: reads the heat map's own shape as before (crisp hotspot vs. washed-out smear) plus, newly, the crop's own raw brightness directly, since that signal is no longer spent on routing between two scorers

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: the existing confusability-weighted place sampler feeds the usual supervised epochs unchanged; a NEW first practice round (the matching game) draws its own pairs -- each step picks two of the six lighting renders and shows the network the SAME set of places under both, each place at its own random heading

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: a brand-new first-stage NT-Xent contrastive loss (reward matching views of the same place, penalize matching different places) runs before the existing grading (reward probability near the true spot, plus confidence); the day/night blend's loss contribution is gone along with the gate

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: a new short practice round (~150 fresh-draw contrastive steps, 128 locations/step) now runs before the usual epoch loop; the usual epochs also treat the freshly pretrained feature extractor less gingerly than the old borrowed one (its own learning rate is now closer to the rest of the network's instead of 10x slower)

implementation_brief:
Preserve the fixed contracts exactly: train.py's CLI (`--area --out-dir --data-dir --epochs --max-crops-per-bucket --seed`) and model/model.py's ONNX export contract (one ONNX per area at <out>/models/<area>.onnx, 1x3x128x128 float32 input in [0,1], output [[u, v, conf]]). Do not touch anything under FROZEN.

=== THE ONE THING THAT SANK EXP 41 -- READ THIS FIRST ===
backbonecheck.py (autoresearch/backbonecheck.py) fingerprints model/model.py and model/train.py by regex, scanning the FULL text including docstrings -- it strips only '#' comments, deliberately NOT triple-quoted strings, so a historical narrative sentence describing a past experiment counts exactly like live code. Run this BEFORE you consider yourself done, and it must print nothing:
  grep -n "mobilenet_v3_small\|mnv3s_features8" model/model.py model/train.py
As of the champion commit there are exactly 5 matching lines: model/model.py lines ~32 (docstring prose: 'Weights load strict from model/pretrained/mnv3s_features8.pt'), ~100 (`from torchvision.models import mobilenet_v3_small`), ~107 (`PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH = ...`), ~121 (`mobilenet_v3_small(weights=None)...`); and model/train.py line ~266 (`"init": "pretrained:mobilenet_v3_small ..."`). Lines 100/107/121 disappear naturally once you delete `_build_pretrained_trunk` and the import (see below). Line 266 disappears because you are replacing that dict value anyway (see below). Line ~32 is the trap: it is prose inside the exp-11 paragraph of the module docstring, untouched by any normal code edit -- you must manually reword that one sentence (keep the historical meaning, e.g. 'Weights loaded strict from a checked-in ImageNet-pretrained checkpoint (verbatim torchvision IMAGENET1K_V1 tensors)' -- just don't spell out the literal old path/identifier) or delete it. Do NOT delete the actual file model/pretrained/mnv3s_features8.pt from disk -- just stop referencing it from code and prose. As a final self-check, run: `.venv/bin/python -m autoresearch.backbonecheck --fingerprint model/model.py model/train.py` -- it must print `scratch`, not the old identifiers.

=== model/model.py ===
1. Remove: `from torchvision.models import mobilenet_v3_small`, `PRETRAINED_TRUNK_PATH`, `IMAGENET_MEAN`, `IMAGENET_STD`, `_build_pretrained_trunk()`, the `norm_mean`/`norm_std` buffers, `dark_logits`, and `gate` (and the `gate_hidden` __init__ arg). Remove `from pathlib import Path` if nothing else in the file uses it.
2. Add a new module-level class `ScratchTrunk(nn.Module)`: four `nn.Sequential` stages, each `Conv2d(in,out,3,stride=2,padding=1) -> BatchNorm2d(out) -> ReLU(inplace=True)`, channel progression 3->32->64->96->64, so 128x128 input yields 64x8x8 output (four stride-2 stages: 128->64->32->16->8). `forward(x)` returns that 8x8x64 map. No normalization buffers needed -- feed raw [0,1] pixels straight in, the stem's BatchNorm layers absorb the input scale.
3. Add a new module-level class `ProjHead(nn.Module)`: `nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(feat_ch, 128), nn.ReLU(inplace=True), nn.Linear(128, 64))` (no normalization inside -- normalize in the loss, not the module). `forward(f)` returns the raw 64-d projection.
4. In `TinyLocNet.__init__`: `self.features = ScratchTrunk()`. Keep the existing `torch.no_grad()` probe pattern to derive `feat_ch`/`fmap_hw` dynamically (will be 64/8) -- don't hardcode. Keep `layout_squeeze = nn.Conv2d(feat_ch, layout_ch, 1)` and a single `self.loc_logits = nn.Linear(feat_ch + layout_ch*fmap_hw*fmap_hw, grid_k*grid_k)` (delete the old `dark_logits`/`gate`, there is now only one field head). Change `self.conf_head` input width to `feat_ch + 4` (GAP descriptor + raw brightness + peak + entropy + sharp/unsharp gap) -- `nn.Sequential(nn.Linear(feat_ch + 4, 32), nn.ReLU(), nn.Linear(32, 1))`. Add `self.proj_head = ProjHead(feat_ch)` (a real submodule, but its `forward` is only ever called from model/train.py's new pretraining code, never from `TinyLocNet.forward` -- so it never appears in the traced ONNX graph and costs nothing in the exported artifact). Keep `conf_shift` buffer and `cell_u`/`cell_v` as-is.
5. Rewrite `TinyLocNet.forward`: `lum = x.mean(dim=(1,2,3)).unsqueeze(1)` (unchanged, still at batch size N); `x4 = _c4_stack(x)` (unchanged); `fmap = self.features(x4)` (no normalization division now -- raw x4 straight into the new trunk); `f4 = fmap.mean(dim=(2,3))`; `layout4 = self.layout_squeeze(fmap).flatten(1)`; `logits4 = self.loc_logits(torch.cat([f4, layout4], dim=1))` (single head, no blend); `logits = logits4.reshape(4, n, -1).mean(dim=0)`; `f = f4.reshape(4, n, -1).mean(dim=0)`. Decode exactly as before (`p = softmax(DECODE_BETA*logits)`, `u,v`; `p1 = softmax(logits)`, `u1,v1`; `peak`, `ent`, `gap` from `p.detach()` as before). Confidence: `z = self.conf_head(torch.cat([f.detach(), lum, peak, ent, gap], dim=1))` (note `lum` is already `[N,1]`, matching `f`'s batch size after the 4-view average -- do NOT repeat it by 4 the way the old gate needed to). `conf = sigmoid(z - self.conf_shift)`. Return `torch.cat([u,v,conf], dim=1)` (plus `logits` if `return_logits`), unchanged shape/contract.
6. `build_model()`, `loss_fn()`, `export_onnx()`, `estimate_position()` are UNCHANGED -- `loss_fn` operates on `pred`/`logits`/`target_uv` only and is architecture-agnostic.
7. Docstring: append a new 'Exp 42:' paragraph (check `sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT MAX(id) FROM experiments;"` and use max+1 if different) summarizing this change, matching the style of the existing Exp N paragraphs. Reword the one offending line in the Exp 11 paragraph per the trap note above.

=== model/train.py ===
1. Add constants: `PRETRAIN_EPOCHS = 3`, `PRETRAIN_STEPS_PER_EPOCH = 50`, `PRETRAIN_BATCH_LOCS = 128`, `PRETRAIN_TEMP = 0.1`, `PRETRAIN_LR = 3e-4`, `TRUNK_LR = 5e-4` (replaces the old hardcoded `1e-4` trunk LR -- the new trunk is contrastively pretrained, not a delicately-borrowed ImageNet model, so it can tolerate a less conservative fine-tune rate than before, though still below the head's `1e-3`).
2. Add `_load_bucket_image(area, data_dir, renders_dir, bucket, r)`: if `r == 0` return `np.asarray(Image.open(area_dir(area, data_dir) / "relight" / f"{bucket}.png"))`, else `np.asarray(Image.open(renders_dir / f"{bucket}_r{r}.png"))`. (You may optionally reuse this from `sample_epoch`/`calibrate_conf_shift` too, but that's not required -- leave those two functions' existing inline loading code alone if touching them adds risk.)
3. Add `nt_xent_loss(za, zb, temp)`: normalize both `[B,64]` tensors with `nn.functional.normalize(..., dim=1)`, concatenate to `z` shape `[2B,64]`, `sim = z @ z.T / temp`, set the diagonal to a large negative number (`sim.fill_diagonal_(-1e9)`), build `targets = torch.cat([torch.arange(B, 2*B), torch.arange(0, B)]).to(z.device)` (index i's positive is its paired view), return `nn.functional.cross_entropy(sim, targets)`.
4. Add `pretrain_contrastive(model, area, data_dir, crops, renders_dir, device, rng) -> dict`: build `opt = torch.optim.Adam(list(model.features.parameters()) + list(model.proj_head.parameters()), lr=PRETRAIN_LR)`. Loop `PRETRAIN_EPOCHS * PRETRAIN_STEPS_PER_EPOCH` steps; each step: pick two DISTINCT buckets `b1, b2` via `rng.choice(list(LIGHTING_BUCKETS), size=2, replace=False)` and independent realizations `r1, r2` via `rng.integers(0, TRAIN_REALIZATIONS)`; load `img1 = _load_bucket_image(area, data_dir, renders_dir, b1, r1)` and `img2 = ...(b2, r2)`; draw `picks = rng.choice(len(crops), size=min(PRETRAIN_BATCH_LOCS, len(crops)), replace=False)`; for each picked crop build `xa.append(extract_crop(img1, c["cx"], c["cy"], float(rng.uniform(0,360))))` and `xb.append(extract_crop(img2, ...,  a different random angle))`; `del img1, img2`; stack, move to device, `.permute(0,3,1,2).contiguous().float().div_(255.0)`; `fa = model.features(xa).mean(dim=(2,3))`, `fb = model.features(xb).mean(dim=(2,3))`; `za = model.proj_head(fa)`, `zb = model.proj_head(fb)`; `loss = nt_xent_loss(za, zb, PRETRAIN_TEMP)`; `opt.zero_grad(); loss.backward(); opt.step()`. Return `{"pretrain_steps": <n>, "pretrain_final_loss": <mean of last 10 step losses>}`. This loads at most 2 full-resolution area images at a time (not all 18 bucket/realization combinations at once) -- keep it that way, this project has hit OOM/ENOSPC before from over-eager caching (see project memory exp33-oom, exp34-enospc).
5. In `train_area`: call `renders_dir = prepare_realizations(...)` (unchanged position), then `model = build_model().to(device)`, then immediately `pretrain_info = pretrain_contrastive(model, area, data_dir, crops, renders_dir, device, rng)` BEFORE the trunk/head optimizer is built. When building `head_params`, also exclude `model.proj_head.parameters()` (by id, same pattern as `trunk_ids`) so the pretraining-only projector doesn't sit in the main optimizer's head group. Change the trunk optimizer group's `"lr"` from `1e-4` to `TRUNK_LR`. Add `pretrain_info["pretrain_steps"]` and `pretrain_info["pretrain_final_loss"]` into the returned `info` dict. Replace the `"init"` dict value with something like `"from-scratch (domain-native NT-Xent contrastive pretraining on the frozen relight pipeline's own multi-lighting/heading renders); no ImageNet, no torchvision backbone, no checkpoint file"` -- must NOT contain the literal substring `mobilenet_v3_small`.
6. `sample_epoch`, `calibrate_conf_shift`, `compute_cell_weights`, `prepare_realizations`, `main()`, and the CLI are otherwise UNCHANGED.
7. Docstring: append a new 'Exp 42:' paragraph describing the pretraining phase, matching the style of existing entries.

=== Final checks before you finish ===
- `grep -n "mobilenet_v3_small\|mnv3s_features8" model/model.py model/train.py` prints nothing.
- `.venv/bin/python -m autoresearch.backbonecheck --fingerprint model/model.py model/train.py` prints `scratch`.
- `.venv/bin/python -c "import model.train, model.model"` succeeds.
- Every non-frozen stage in this design's `architecture.stages` has a real corresponding code diff (they all should, given the above).
